A1: To be affirmative — is/am/are in positive form
To be interrogative — asking questions with "be"
Present simple affirmative — do/does for routines
Future simple affirmative — will for future plans
Future simple negative — won't for future negation
Prepositions of time — in/on/at for time
Prepositions of place — in/on/under for location
Must for obligation — rules or necessity
This / that / these / those — pointing to things
Was / were — past form of be
Telling time — saying hours and minutes
Telling numbers — counting and reading numbers
Very vs really — degree of intensity
Present continuous negative — not happening now
A2: Question words — asking for information
How questions — asking "how" things happen
Subject and object questions — identifying subject or object in questions (Who called? / Who did you call?)
Adverbs of frequency — how often something happens (always, often, never)
Present simple — habits, routines, facts
Past simple — completed actions in the past
Future simple — will for future plans
Simple tenses — present, past, and future basic forms
Present continuous — actions happening now
Present continuous for future — planned future events
Past continuous — actions in progress in the past
Present perfect — experiences and results up to now
Time markers — words showing when (yesterday, just, already)
Auxiliary verbs — helping verbs (do, be, have)
Prepositions of time — in/on/at for time
Prepositions of place — in/on/under for location
Adjectives and prepositions — tired of / interested in
Verbs and prepositions — depend on / focus on
Comparatives — comparing two things (bigger, faster)
Superlatives — the highest degree (the biggest)
Be going to — future intentions and plans
Would like — polite requests and wishes
So / such — emphasis in sentences
Too / enough — expressing excess or sufficiency
B1: Question words — asking for information
Subject and object questions — identifying subject or object (Who called? / Who did you call?)
Present simple vs continuous — routines vs actions now
Past simple vs continuous — finished actions vs actions in progress
Used to / would — talking about past habits
Present perfect simple — experiences and recent actions
Present perfect continuous — duration of actions starting in the past
Past perfect — actions happening before another past action
Future forms — will, going to, present continuous for future
Future continuous — actions in progress at a future time
Modal verbs of ability — can, could, be able to
Modal verbs of obligation — must, have to, should
Modal verbs of possibility — may, might, could
Conditionals 0 & 1 — facts and likely future results
Conditional 2 — hypothetical present or future situations
Passive voice — focusing on the action, not the doer
Reported speech — saying what someone else said
Relative clauses — adding info with who, which, that, where
Comparatives and superlatives — more complex comparisons
Gerunds and infinitives — verbs ending in -ing or starting with to
Question tags — checking information (don't you? isn't it?)
Countable and uncountable nouns — complex quantities
Quantifiers — both, either, neither, each, every
Prepositions after verbs & adjectives — depend on, interested in
B2: All narrative tenses — past simple, continuous, perfect simple & continuous
Future perfect — actions finished by a certain future time
Future perfect continuous — duration up to a future point
Mixed conditionals — linking past actions to present results
Conditionals 3 — hypothetical past situations and regrets
Passive voice in all tenses — including modal passives
Passive with reporting verbs — It is said that / He is thought to
Causative have / get — having something done for you
Reported speech — complex shifts and reporting verbs (suggest, deny)
Gerunds vs infinitives — subtle differences in meaning (remember to vs remember doing)
Relative clauses — defining vs non-defining
Modal verbs of deduction — must have, can’t have, might have
Wish / If only — expressing regrets and desires
I’d rather / It’s time — specific structures for preference and urgency
Inversion for emphasis — Never have I seen… / Not only… but also
Participle clauses — Replacing clauses with -ing or -ed forms
Articles — advanced usage and omissions
Noun phrases — complex structures with adjectives and nouns
Discourse markers — linking ideas in formal and informal speech
Adverbs and adverbial phrases — position and meaning
C1: rather / had better — advice
Bare infinitive — make, let, help
Complex object — I want you to help me
See / hear / watch somebody do / doing — perception verbs
Phrasal verbs — advanced multi-word verbs
The… the… comparative — The sooner, the better
Can’t help + -ing — I can’t help laughing
Seem like / look like / feel like / sound like — structures
Dare — as modal & main verb
Nominalisation — the implementation of a policy
Participle clauses — Knowing the risks, she still…
Fronting — What I need most is sleep
Cleft sentences — It was John who broke the vase
Complex relative clauses — the man whose car was stolen…
Reduced relative clauses — The man standing over there
Discourse markers — as a matter of fact, on the whole
Parallel structures — not only… but also…
Advanced comparatives — far better, much less difficult
Inversion after negative adverbials — Under no circumstances should…
Advanced reported speech — mixing direct and indirect
Complex conditional phrases — Supposing that…, Provided that…
